markdown:
#  Review RFQ Responses 
##  Background 
When a Change Event affects a Commitment, your project team has the option to create an RFQ to obtain a quote from a collaborator on your project. See [Create RFQs from a Change Event](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/create-rfqs-from-a-change-event). When adding a collaborator's quote to Procore, your team has these options:
  * Your team can assign an RFQ to a collaborator and then send it to the collaborator email. See [Assign an RFQ](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/assign-and-send-an-rfq-to-a-collaborator). Next, your collaborator can submit their quote as described in [Submit a Quote as a Collaborator](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/submit-a-quote-as-a-collaborator). Then, you can proceed with the steps below. 
  * If your team doesn't want to grant collaborators the permissions they need to submit a quote directly in Procore, you can manage the quote submission process outside of Procore. If you choose this option, you will want to enter the quote you received into Procore as described in [Respond to an RFQ on Behalf of a Collaborator](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/respond-to-an-rfq-on-behalf-of-a-collaborator). Then, you can proceed with the steps below.


##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * To review an RFQ response, 'Read Only' level permissions or higher on the project's Change Events tool and access to view the related commitment.
    * To change the status of the RFQ, you must have 'Admin' level permissions on the project's Change Events tool. 
#####  Tip
**Want to view RFQ responses from the Commitments tool?** If so, follow the steps in [View a Purchase Order](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/view-a-purchase-order) or [View a Subcontract](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/view-a-subcontract) and then click the **RFQs** tab.


##  Steps 
Choose from these methods to review responses:
  * Review Responses from the RFQs Tab
  * Review Responses from the Detail Tab


### Review Responses from the RFQs Tab
  1. Navigate to the project's **Change Events** tool.
  2. Click the **RFQs** tab.
  3. Scroll to find the change event with RFQs you want to review, or use the filters to narrow your search. See [View, Filter, and Search RFQs](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/view-filter-and-search-rfqs). ​Note: You can tell if an RFQ has been responded to if the following are true:
     * The 'Current Quote' field shows the amount the subcontractor entered in the RFQ response.  
_Note_ : You can click the RFQ to review additional information within the actual RFQ.
     * The status shows _Under Review_ , _Pending Final Approval_ , or _Closed_.
  4. (_Optional_) After reviewing RFQ, it is recommended that you update its status to _Pending Final Approval_. This allows you to apply the RFQ filter to see which RFQs have already been reviewed and which are still under review.


### Review Responses from the Detail Tab
  1. Navigate to the project's **Change Events** tool.
  2. Scroll to find the change event with the RFQs you want to review, or use the filters to narrow your search. See [View, Filter, and Search RFQs](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/view-filter-and-search-rfqs).  
_Note_ : You can tell if an RFQ has been responded to if the following are true:
     * The RFQ column shows the amount the subcontractor entered in the RFQ response.  
_Note_ : You can click the RFQ amount to review additional information within the actual RFQ.
     * The status shows _Under Review_ , _Pending Final Approval_ , or _Closed_. You can hover over the RFQ amount to see the status.
  3. (_Optional_) After reviewing RFQ, it is recommended that you update its status to _Pending Final Approval_. This allows you to apply the RFQ filter to see which RFQs have already been reviewed and which are still under review.


## See Also
  * [Change Events - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/permissions)
  * [Respond to an RFQ on Behalf of a Collaborator](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/respond-to-an-rfq-on-behalf-of-a-collaborator)
  * [Change Events - Overview](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project)


A _Change Event_ is any event resulting in a modification that affects the _scope of work_ on a construction project and the project's schedule and/or cost(s). In Procore, a change event can be recorded in the system and precedes the creation of a _change order_ , which allows your team members and stakeholders to prepare for a cost change before it becomes an actual cost.
In Procore, a _Commitment_ is a _purchase order_ or _subcontract_. Both commitment types are contracts that represent a legally enforceable financial agreement between two parties.
title: Review RFQ Responses
description: How to review a quote submitted by a collaborator in response to a Request for Quote (RFQ).
